description: Multi-model /team-review FAIL re-review cycle. Cycle 1 produces FAIL with 2 findings (1 fixed by author, 1 disputed). Cycle 2 re-runs orchestrator on patched diff: orchestrator re-fans-out to native team + external adapters; consolidation produces a new report. Verifies multi-model FAIL re-review behavior: orchestrator re-runs at each cycle (~2-3× per-cycle token cost vs native-only), Lead re-publishes verbatim each cycle.

raw_findings.title: Transaction does not rollback on partial failure
raw_findings.description: If step 2 of the transaction fails, step 1's writes are not rolled back. Leaves DB in inconsistent state.

raw_findings.title: Transaction connection not released on early return
raw_findings.description: Early-return paths skip the connection.release() call. Will exhaust the connection pool under load.

findings.consolidation_notes: Minority-of-one demotion: medium → low (single source, non-security).

cycle_2.patched_diff: executeBatch wrapped in try/catch with explicit rollback; connection.release() moved to finally block.

raw_findings.title: Transaction rollback now correctly applied
raw_findings.description: Previously raised in cycle 1; verified fixed in cycle 2 by code-reviewer.

consolidated_output.consolidation_notes: Cycle 1's HIGH finding verified-fixed in cycle 2 (info-level acknowledgment, no carry-over). Performance finding from cycle 1 also resolved.

verification_assertions: Orchestrator was re-invoked at cycle 2 (FR-MMT3 multi-model FAIL re-review)
Lead published cycle 2's consolidated_output verbatim, not an aggregate of cycle 1 + cycle 2
Per-cycle token cost ~2-3× native-only baseline (documented in plan task 15 acceptance)
Verdict transition fail → pass terminated the cycle
